description_long: This is a mid-rise apartment building with a rectangular footprint, featuring a flat black roof and a predominantly white facade accented by red horizontal bands and window sills. The front and rear elevations are symmetrical, each with four floors of regularly spaced windows and red awnings over the balconies. The building has a prominent central entrance with a small staircase leading up to it, and the ground floor is distinguished by a solid red base. Balconies with red awnings are present on both the front and rear sides, providing outdoor space for several units. The side elevations are plain, with only windows and no balconies or decorative elements. The overall design is functional and utilitarian, typical of post-war or Soviet-era residential architecture.
description: A four-story rectangular apartment building with a flat black roof, white facade, red accents, and balconies with red awnings on the front and rear sides.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance, four floors of windows, and four red-awning balconies on each side.
description_view_1: The left side view displays a plain wall with regularly spaced windows and no balconies or entrance.
description_view_2: The rear view mirrors the front, with four floors of windows and four red-awning balconies, but no visible entrance.
description_view_3: The right side view is identical to the left, featuring only windows and no balconies or entrance.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical columns and horizontal rows, evenly spaced across each floor, with each column aligning perfectly from top to bottom.
